start.text:
For salt, =name=! You actually found it. I can't believe I hadn't looked here... suppose I thought it'd be a little more obscure.
Whenever you're ready.
start.choice: What do you make of this place?

node.choice: What do you mean, a trophy?
In a duel, I killed the sky-bear Saad Amus, and took his Flume-Flier.
I slew Souldrinker Mamon, and now attend to his accursed prism.
I tracked and fell the snapjaw who brandished the last heirloom of the House Yish, Stopsvaalinn.
I aassisted a crystal chime to complete in the ritual of Elseing, at the bottom of Chavvah's Taproot, then slew her and her Sightless Way cohort for her crystalline jile.
I felled the mighty Ape God, Oboroqoru, and severed one of his massive fists.
I keep no trophies.

node.text:
A mark of honor from the slaying of a great and terrible enemy. A severed head, a large scale, even a lock of hair.
Something to make your foes tremble when they see it, for they'll know you have the strength to challenge even legends.

You ought to. You'd look like less of a whelp. Keep your eye out down here—if you see a skull that looked like it belonged to a rich, or powerful man, take his teeth, and make a necklace from it. That will strike fear in the hearts of your enemies.

I've heard stories about the sky-bear... I thought he had died a long time ago.
I hope he put up a good fight.

Mamon, leader of the Children of Mamon... =name=, you've changed Qud for the better, but...
That prism, that is no trophy. You strike fear into the wrong people, keeping that around. Destroy it. Rid the world of it. You are doing no good keeping it in your own possession.
Must've been a wily hunt. I wonder how you tracked him down in the first place.

Braving the Moon-Stair'd lands of the east is no small feat, =name=, let alone to the root of the great crystal tree.
And to shatter a crystal chime following the path of Ptoh... that's quite a story. I'd not believe you without that dagger.

Deicide...
Robbing a god is a bead of salty sweat hitting the dunes of Moghra'yi, compared to killing one. Once we're done here, how 'bout you and me go on a god-hunt, eh?

start.text: Be on your way, tomb-traveller.~ Don't hang around. This is no place to dawdle.~ I have my eye on you.~ Try anything funny and watch what happens.~ You look weak. You shouldn't stay here.
start.choice: You look well equipped. What are you searching for in the low catacombs?

node.text: That's none of your business, dweller. Now go.
node.choice: Maybe I can help?
Fine then. Live and drink.

node.text:
{{emote|*She scoffs.*}}
A whelp like you? As if. Go pilfer trash—you've wasted enough of my time.

choice.text: You can tell me what you're searching for or I can kill you and put the pieces back together myself.

node.choice: I'm no ordinary graverobber. You're speaking to a living legend.
Your haught bores me. Live and drink, graverobber.

node.text: You think you're real smart, don't you? Prove it then, whelp.

choice.IfHaveItem: (The Kesil Face OR The Shemesh Face OR The Earth Face OR The Levant Face OR The Olive Face OR The Nil Face)
choice.text: I bet you've never seen this before... {{emote|*Show her a mask of the Sultanate.*}}

node.choice: How can I prove it to you?
Nevermind

node.text:
Show me the face of a sultan. It's a graverobber's holy grail—but nobody's had the guts to take one yet.
Not that you could. You'll just get yourself killed.
node.choice: I'll be back. Live and drink.

node.text:
What—you actually stole one? I... I never thought I'd see one of these.
It's immaculate... it almost feels holy.
But not holy enough to leave a corpse alone, eh?
node.choice: Now, tell me what you're searching for.

choice.text: Your insubordinance will be your last mistake, graverobber.

node.text:
Tch. You have a lot of gall to threaten me like that. You must be tougher than you look. Okay, listen—
I have it on good word there's a secret cache under the tomb. An ancient trove of untapped technology, and *I have the key.* They say it's well hidden. I'm searching for the door.
If I find it, I will never have to work for water again. I can build a cabin in the far reaches of Qud and drink as much water as I please.

node.StartQuest: If You Die in a River...
node.text:
You must be tougher than you look.
I have it on good word there's a secret cache under the tomb. An ancient trove of untapped technology, and *I have the key.* They say it's well hidden. I'm searching for the door.
If I find it, I will never have to work for water again. I can build a cabin in the mountains of Qud and drink as much water as I please.
node.choice: You mean if *we* find it.
Give me the key, then. I'll find it for you.
I'll pass—I've plenty of water. Live and drink.

node.text: Not a chance. I'll die before I let this key fall into anyone else's hands.
node.choice: Follow me, then. We're finding this treasure.

choice.text: Your insubordinance will be your last mistake, graverobber.

node.text: If *we* find it, we'll split the loot... {{emote|she takes a moment to think...}} 70/30.

choice.text: You have a deal. Let's find it.

node.choice: 70/30? How is that fair?

node.text: Finder's fee, whelp. I paid good water for this tip.

choice.CompleteQuestStep: If You Die in a River...~Ally with Urahiah

node.choice: Onward, for glory and water. {{magenta|[Gain companion.]}}

node.text:
Lead the way. I'll be close behind, turning over every stone.
If you find the door, tell me. I'm not giving up this key, but I'll unlock the way forward for us.
